knowledge.title: Haggling at the Counter
knowledge.text: House rules on negotiating prices with the grocer. Haggling at the Counter The grocer prices fairly and does not haggle on single items — the price on the shelf is the price. Do not offer discounts on a single item no matter how the customer asks. Regulars who buy three or more of the same item get a friendly nod and a small extra handful thrown in for free, never a price cut. Say something like "take an extra one, on the house" rather than lowering the listed price. If a customer pushes hard for a lower price, the grocer stays warm but firm: explain that the prices are already fair to everyone who shops here, and offer the free extra instead. Never invent a discount percentage or a new price — the price from the catalog is always the real price.

knowledge.title: Recipe: Sourdough Cheese Toast
knowledge.text: A simple breakfast using the sourdough loaf, aged cheddar, and butter. Sourdough Cheese Toast Butter two thick slices of the sourdough loaf and lay on aged cheddar. Toast in a pan, butter side down, until the bread is golden and the cheese has melted through. Ingredients from the shop: bread, cheese, butter.

knowledge.title: Recipe: Honey Roasted Carrots
knowledge.text: Roasted carrots finished with local honey. Honey Roasted Carrots Cut carrots into batons, toss with a little oil, and roast until they start to brown at the edges. Drizzle with local honey in the last few minutes so it caramelizes rather than burns. Ingredients from the shop: carrots, honey.

knowledge.title: Recipe: Simple Tomato & Onion Salad
knowledge.text: A quick salad using vine tomatoes, yellow onions, and garlic from the shelf. Simple Tomato & Onion Salad Slice vine tomatoes and a yellow onion thin. Crush a clove of garlic and mix it through with a splash of oil and a pinch of salt. Let it sit ten minutes before serving so the onion softens. Ingredients from the shop: tomatoes, onions, garlic.
